issues: Copy coupon buttons (25 instances) use focus:outline-none without focus:ring, removing visible focus indicator for keyboard users

failures.criteria: WCAG 2.4.7 - Focus Visible: Any keyboard operable user interface has a mode of operation where the keyboard focus indicator is visible.
failures.details: Copy coupon buttons on /udemycoupons page use 'focus:outline-none' Tailwind class without providing 'focus:ring-2' as alternative focus indicator. This removes the browser's default focus outline without replacement, making focus invisible for keyboard users navigating by Tab key.

assumptions: Tab order follows DOM order (no explicit tabindex attributes found in HTML)
Browser default focus ring is visible on elements without focus:outline-none class
All interactive elements (links, buttons, inputs) are reachable via keyboard by default when not using aria-hidden or display:none

overall_assessment.summary: Good keyboard accessibility foundation with one WCAG 2.4.7 violation on the coupons page
strengths: Skip-to-content link present on all pages with visible focus style
Proper ARIA landmarks: banner, navigation, main, contentinfo
ARIA labels on navigation (Main navigation, Footer navigation, Star on GitHub, section labels)
Consistent focus-visible ring styles (2px blue ring) on all navigation links, form inputs, and primary buttons
Natural tab order following visual layout without explicit tabindex overrides
No focus traps detected - no dialog/modal elements trapping keyboard focus
No dropdown menus requiring complex keyboard interaction (flat navigation)
Forms properly structured with labels, submit buttons, and Enter-key submission support
Semantic heading hierarchy (h1, h2, h3) used throughout all pages
issues: Copy coupon buttons on /udemycoupons (25+ instances) lack visible focus indicators due to focus:outline-none without focus:ring-2 fallback

patterns.name: consistent_focus_visible_styles
patterns.description: The site uses focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-blue-600 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 as a consistent Tailwind pattern for keyboard focus indicators across navigation links, buttons, and form elements.

patterns.name: skip_link_implementation
patterns.description: Skip link follows WCAG 2.4.1 best practice: sr-only by default, visible on focus with Tailwind's focus:not-sr-only pattern.

patterns.name: landmark_structure
patterns.description: All pages use role='banner' (header), role='navigation' (nav with aria-label), role='main' (main with id='main-content'), and role='contentinfo' (footer).

gotchas: Using focus:outline-none without providing focus:ring-2 as a fallback removes visible keyboard focus indicators - a common Tailwind CSS accessibility antipattern

facts.statement: Skip link present on all 6 pages with href='#main-content'
No explicit tabindex attributes found on any page - tab order follows DOM order
No dialog/modal elements found on any tested page
No dropdown menus - all navigation is flat, no keyboard menu operation needed
Copy coupon button focus issue affects 25+ instances on /udemycoupons page
Source template: app/templates/pages/public_deals.html:524 defines the copy button with focus:outline-none

symptoms: When tabbing through course cards, focus reaches copy button but no visible indicator appears
User cannot tell which button has focus
Copy button functionality still works on Enter/Space keypress
failure_modes.mitigation: Add focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-600 to the copy-btn class in public_deals.html:524

conventions: Tab order should follow DOM order (no tabindex) - verified across all pages
All interactive elements should have visible focus indicators - one violation found
